PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2021 Continued To Oct-18-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady appeared by zoom for Richard Koch.

Mr. O'Grady requested a 30-day continuance stating that the two-year accounting has not been filed because the Probate Referee is revising the inventory and appraisal. The Seventh Report on Status of Administration is continued to October 18, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-14-2021 Continued To Aug-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady and Rosemary LaPuma, via ZOOM, counsel for Richard Koch, Personal Representative.

The court begins by advising counsel that a two-year accounting is due beginning the date of death, June 15, 2014. Mr. O'Grady reports that he had submitted an accounting report along with a supplement but will file a separate accounting to cover the period requested by the court. The court then reminds counsel that if the estate remains insolvent, he must notify any unpaid creditors with the accounting hearing date.

The Status on Accounting is continued to August 16, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-10-2021 Continued To Jun-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Quiet Title In Personal Property In Possession Of Decedent
Off Calendar Apr-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Lawrence Townsend appeared by zoom for Bohemian Club (Petitioner).
Rauya Mhtar appeared by telephone for USC.
Richard Koch appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that four folks were potentially in possession of the ledger at various times, but only two are respondents. Mr. Townsend stated that Mr. Wilson passed away in 2017, that Mr. Linder never actually had possession of the ledger and that Mr. Linder was consulted to make a sale, but he never had possession. Mr. Koch stated that Mr. Linder took it upon himself to offer the book for sale. The Court noted that Mr. Linder had detailed information. Mr. Townsend stated that Mr. Linder never wrote the advertisement, that Mr. Koch's position is that he gave the ledger to USC, that USC's position is that it does not have it, and that he does not think there was ever any dispute that the ledger was in the possession of the estate. Mr. Koch stated that he does not object to the petition itself, but he does not agree with a surcharge and read a letter to the Court regarding the removal of the property. The Court informed Mr. Koch that he needs to file the letter with the Court. Ms. Mhtar stated that USC's position is that they don't currently have possession of the ledger, that they never had possession and that they are not aware of the location of the ledger. The Court raised the issue of deferring the surcharge issue for now. Mr. Townsend stated that he needs to conduct more discovery in order to answer the Court's questions, that they are doing an investigation outside of Court, and that he needs to do a document request from USC and take the deposition of Mr. Koch. All of the parties before the Court indicated that they do not know where the ledger is. The Court raised the issue of the Bohemian Club having the first right of ownership of the ledger. Mr. Koch and Ms. Mhtar both indicated that they do not object. The Petition to Quiet Title in Personal Property in Possession of Decedent is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions Allowing Executor To Abandon Certain Assets As Uncollectible
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John O'Grady for and with Richard Koch (Petitioner).

The Court questions why the Parties are here, explained that the ledger and the debt can be dealt with under other procedures in the Probate Code, referred to and read language in Probate Code 9611, and stated that it believes both of these assets fall under the code. Mr. O'Grady stated that he wasn't aware of these code sections and that neither were any of his Colleagues. The Petition for Instructions Allowing Executor to Abandon Certain Assets as Uncollectable is denied without prejudice. Mr. O'Grady is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Pamela Louie for Richard Koch (Executor).

The Court questions why it is taking so long to sell the art. Ms. Louie stated that the Estate has about one thousand items of unique character, that they have been difficult to sell and that it took over one year to get appraisals. Ms. Louie also stated that there are Russian religious items remaining and that the Executor hopes to have them sold by the end of the month. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration (subject to review of the order). Status on close of the estate is set for October 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on sale of the remaining items.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
